message:
chore: Sync beads - close bd-1jt.3.4 (SBOM), bd-ov2 (coverage)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.11","title":"Add pre-release quality gates (typecheck/lint/test) per tool","description":"# Add Pre-Release Quality Gates (typecheck/lint/test) per Tool

## Purpose
Ensure every release passes the tool's own quality checks before upload to GitHub.

## Behavior
- Read `checks` from `repos.d/<tool>.yaml`
- Run checks in deterministic order
- Support `--skip-checks` and `--dry-run`
- Log per-check duration + exit code
- Fail release if any check fails (exit 1/6 depending on stage)

## Output
- stderr: human-readable summary
- stdout (JSON): list of checks, durations, exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checks run per-tool before release upload
- [ ] Failures block release with clear errors
- [ ] JSON output includes per-check results
- [ ] Dry-run shows planned checks","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:06.086111454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:27:28.402142395Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:27:28.402125253Z","close_reason":"Implemented src/quality_gates.sh module with qg_run_checks() and qg_get_checks(). Added 'dsr quality' command with --dry-run, --skip-checks, --work-dir options. Reads checks from repos.yaml, logs per-check results with durations, returns JSON with passed/failed counts. Exit 1 on failure.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:50:21.256627491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:47.402939311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:06.086111454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.2","title":"Update Homebrew and Scoop formulas after release","description":"# Update Homebrew and Scoop Formulas After Release

## Purpose
Update package manager metadata so users can install via `brew` and `scoop` with correct URLs + SHA256.

## Homebrew
- Update formula in `homebrew-tap`
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp` (never `/data/projects`)
- Validate with `brew audit --strict` and `brew style` when available
- Respect `--dry-run` (plan only)

## Scoop
- Update manifest JSON in `scoop-bucket`
- Use `sha256:<hex>` format
- Use temp workdir in `/tmp`
- Validate JSON and run `scoop checkver` when available

## Logging / Output
- stderr: human-readable summary
- stdout: JSON (when `--json`) listing updated formula/manifest paths + checksums

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Formula/manifest updated with correct version + hashes
- [ ] Dry-run available and deterministic
- [ ] Validation commands executed when available
- [ ] Works for all tools without touching `/data/projects`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:34.632986314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:51:41.535614972Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:51:41.535564347Z","close_reason":"Implemented cmd_release_formulas in src/release_formulas.sh with Homebrew formula and Scoop manifest updates. Features: platform detection, checksum extraction from release assets, git clone to temp dirs (never /data/projects), brew audit validation, jq manifest updates, --push flag, --dry-run support, JSON output.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:34.632986314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:26:56.108363071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:39:56.629546588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.3","title":"Implement minisign artifact signing","description":"# Implement Minisign Artifact Signing

## Purpose
Sign every release artifact so users can verify authenticity.

## Requirements
- Sign every binary + checksums.sha256
- Store signatures as `.minisig` files
- Use password-protected key from secrets store

## Installer Integration
- Verify signatures when minisign present
- Fail when `--require-signatures` is set

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All binaries + checksum file signed
- [ ] Signatures uploaded with releases
- [ ] Installers verify signatures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:59.730105373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:16:01.600966648Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:16:01.600949125Z","close_reason":"Minisign signing module complete in src/signing.sh: keypair generation (init), configuration check, single file signing (sign), batch signing (sign_batch), signature verification (verify), public key extraction (pubkey), permission fixing (fix). CLI exposed via 'dsr signing' subcommand. Integration with release/installer commands will happen in bd-1jt.3.1 and bd-1jt.4.x.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:23:59.730105373Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:24:03.620154033Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Produce SBOMs for each release to improve supply-chain transparency.

## Formats
- SPDX JSON
- CycloneDX JSON

## Behavior
- Generate SBOMs after build, before release
- Attach as release assets
- For container images, attach SBOM attestation if enabled

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SBOMs generated for each release
- [ ] Uploaded to GitHub release
- [ ] Works for Rust/Go/Bun projects","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:31.491498168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:04:14.307234787Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:47.963689719Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:31.491498168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.5","title":"Implement checksum auto-sync across flywheel repos","description":"# Checksum Auto-Sync Across Flywheel Repos

## Purpose
Update checksum manifests in downstream repos when installers change.

## Behavior
- Triggered after dsr release
- Recalculate checksums, update target repo in /tmp (never `/data/projects`)
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations
- For external tools, open a security review issue instead of auto-merge

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checksums recalculated after each release
- [ ] External changes flagged for review
- [ ] JSON output for verification","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:44.275881995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:53:30.421759326Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:44.275881995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.145176916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.6","title":"Implement SLSA provenance attestation for supply chain security","description":"# Implement SLSA Provenance Attestation

## Purpose
Generate SLSA v1 provenance (in-toto Statement + predicate) for release artifacts.

## Required Fields
- `_type`: in-toto Statement v1
- `predicateType`: https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1
- `subject[]`: artifact name + sha256 digest
- `predicate.buildDefinition`: buildType + externalParameters
- `predicate.runDetails`: builder id + timestamps + invocation id

## Behavior
- Generate provenance for each release (one per tool/version)
- Attach as release asset
- For containers, attach cosign attestation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Provenance generated for each artifact set
- [ ] Attached to GitHub release
- [ ] Verifiable with standard SLSA tooling","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:38.736005543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:58:38.288053284Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.805508188Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:38.736005543Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.3.7","title":"Implement repository dispatch for cross-repo coordination","description":"# Repository Dispatch Coordination

## Purpose
Trigger downstream workflows after dsr release (checksums, formulas, canaries).

## Requirements
- Use authenticated gh API with correct scopes
- Include idempotent payload (tool, version, sha, run_id)
- Log all dispatches in structured logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dispatches sent to all target repos
- [ ] Payload includes tool/version/sha/run_id
- [ ] Failures are retried with backoff","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:07.721230129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:58:37.693873953Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:07.721230129Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.3.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:49.136236393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -46,7 +46,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.2","title":"Implement toolchain version detection and auto-installation in installers","description":"# Toolchain Version Detection and Auto-Installation

## Purpose
Detect missing/outdated toolchains and offer to install them **without** breaking existing setups.

## Rules
- Never overwrite existing toolchains without explicit user consent
- Never auto-delete existing installs (print instructions instead)
- Respect `--non-interactive` (fail with actionable message)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detects existing installations correctly
- [ ] Version checks work on all platforms
- [ ] Installation does not break existing setups
- [ ] Clear manual upgrade instructions provided","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:18:07.405296669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:07:18.386321869Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:07:18.386298995Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added portable pure-bash version comparison (no sort -V), jq/curl dependency checks. All detection functions working, safety rules enforced.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:18:07.405296669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.3","title":"Add minisign verification to install.sh (optional but recommended)","description":"# Add Minisign Verification to install.sh (Optional but Recommended)

## Purpose
Verify downloaded binaries with minisign signatures to prevent tampering.

## Behavior
- If minisign available: verify by default
- If missing: warn, optionally install, or fail when --require-signatures
- Embed public key in installer (from dsr config)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] install.sh verifies .minisig when available
- [ ] Clear failure if signature invalid
- [ ] Non-interactive mode supports --require-signatures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:11.776660104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:13:27.576956969Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:13:27.576911403Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added _verify_minisign() function to install.sh template. Supports --require-signatures flag, embeds public key from config, downloads .minisig files, verifies with minisign tool.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.206972391Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.459042194Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:11.776660104Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:57.697458578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.4.4","title":"Add installer cache/offline mode + gh release download fallback","description":"# Add Installer Cache/Offline Mode + gh release download Fallback

## Purpose
Improve reliability when network is flaky by caching release assets and allowing offline installs.

## Behavior
- Cache downloads in ~/.cache/dsr/installers/<tool>/<version>
- Support --cache-dir and --offline flags
- Prefer gh release download when gh is available (auth + retries)
- Fallback to curl when gh is missing

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Cached assets reused when available
- [ ] Offline mode fails fast with clear error if cache missing
- [ ] gh release download works for private repos","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:19.727170447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T22:29:19.781146281Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T22:29:19.781128417Z","close_reason":"Implemented cache directory support (~/.cache/dsr/installers/<tool>/<version>), --cache-dir and --offline flags, auto-caching on download, and gh release download fallback for private repos. Added test_install_gen.sh with 9 passing tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:02.979791158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:19.727170447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:04.030679358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.4.5","title":"Provide PowerShell installer wrapper for native Windows","description":"# Provide PowerShell Installer Wrapper for Native Windows

## Purpose
Offer a first-class Windows install path (install.ps1) that mirrors install.sh behavior for users not using WSL.

## Behavior
- Detect OS/arch, download matching .exe
- Verify SHA256 (and minisign if available)
- Install to user-writable PATH (e.g., %LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\dsr\bin)
- Support `-Yes`, `-Verbose`, `-Version`, `-Prefix`, `-CacheDir`, `-Offline`
- Respect non-interactive mode (`$env:CI=1` or `-Yes`)

## Logging
- Human-readable logs to stderr
- Optional JSON output for automation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] install.ps1 parity with install.sh (flags, logs)
- [ ] Works on Windows 10/11 without admin
- [ ] Signature verification supported when minisign present
- [ ] Documented usage in README","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:25.838673495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:52:27.874310491Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:39:03.227926195Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:37:25.838673495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.359019185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.618811604Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.4.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:40:08.857268243Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1jt.4.5","title":"Provide PowerShell installer wrapper for native Windows","description":"# Provide PowerShell Installer Wrapper for Native Windows

Offer a first-class Windows install path (install.ps1) that mirrors install.sh behavior for users not using WSL.

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5","title":"Theme 5: Testing & Validation","description":"# Testing & Validation

## Purpose
Ensure all components of the fallback release system work correctly through comprehensive unit, integration, and E2E tests with detailed logging.

## Testing Strategy

### 1. Unit Tests
- Platform detection
- Release freshness calculation
- Checksum/signature verification
- Toolchain detection and guardrails

### 2. Integration Tests
- Check → Build → Release pipeline (mocked GH/SSH)
- Repos registry management
- Status/report + prune dry-run

### 3. Contract Tests
- JSON schema validation for **every** `--json` output
- Stream separation: stderr empty on success

### 4. E2E Tests
- Docker containers for Linux installers
- Act-based fallback pipeline (mocked release)
- Package manager update workflow (Homebrew/Scoop) in dry-run

## Test Framework
Use bats-core for Bash tests.

## Logging Requirements
All tests must capture and print logs on failure (shared harness).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit + integration tests run <5 minutes
- [ ] E2E tests run <30 minutes
- [ ] JSON schema + stream separation validated
- [ ] Detailed logs available for failures","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:10:44.485219623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T21:34:57.211188096Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:10:44.485219623Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.1","title":"Write unit tests for platform detection and freshness checking","description":"# Unit Tests: Platform Detection & Freshness Checking

## Test File
tests/unit/test_core_functions.bats

## Platform Detection Tests

### Test Cases
\`\`\`bash
@test "detect Linux x86_64" {
  mock_uname "Linux" "x86_64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "linux-amd64"
}

@test "detect Linux ARM64" {
  mock_uname "Linux" "aarch64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "linux-arm64"
}

@test "detect macOS ARM64 (Apple Silicon)" {
  mock_uname "Darwin" "arm64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "darwin-arm64"
}

@test "detect macOS x86_64 (Intel)" {
  mock_uname "Darwin" "x86_64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "darwin-amd64"
}

@test "detect Windows via MINGW" {
  mock_uname "MINGW64_NT-10.0" "x86_64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_equal "\$PLATFORM" "windows-amd64"
}

@test "unsupported OS fails gracefully" {
  mock_uname "FreeBSD" "amd64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "Unsupported OS"
}

@test "unsupported arch fails gracefully" {
  mock_uname "Linux" "riscv64"
  run detect_platform
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "Unsupported architecture"
}
\`\`\`

## Freshness Check Tests

### Test Cases
\`\`\`bash
@test "release 0 commits behind is fresh" {
  mock_gh_compare 0
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_success
}

@test "release 10 commits behind is fresh (boundary)" {
  mock_gh_compare 10
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_success
}

@test "release 11 commits behind is stale (boundary)" {
  mock_gh_compare 11
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "commits behind"
}

@test "release 100 commits behind is very stale" {
  mock_gh_compare 100
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "100 commits behind"
}

@test "freshness check handles API error" {
  mock_gh_error "rate limit exceeded"
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  # Should warn but not fail hard
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Could not check freshness"
}

@test "freshness check without gh CLI uses curl fallback" {
  unset_command "gh"
  mock_curl_compare 5
  run check_freshness "v1.0.0"
  assert_success
}
\`\`\`

## Mock Helpers
\`\`\`bash
# tests/helpers/mocks.bash

mock_uname() {
  local os="\$1" arch="\$2"
  function uname() {
    case "\$1" in
      -s) echo "\$os" ;;
      -m) echo "\$arch" ;;
    esac
  }
  export -f uname
}

mock_gh_compare() {
  local ahead_by="\$1"
  function gh() {
    if [[ "\$*" == *"compare"* ]]; then
      echo "{\"ahead_by\": \$ahead_by}"
    fi
  }
  export -f gh
}

mock_gh_error() {
  local error="\$1"
  function gh() {
    echo "gh: \$error" >&2
    return 1
  }
  export -f gh
}
\`\`\`
